title: Blind scope commitments against bitmask brute-force

artifacts.description: Replace Poseidon(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment) with Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt); add blindingSalt as a private signal input
Update chain-linking check to recompute scopeCommitment using the 3-ary hash; thread blindingSalt as private input through delegation chain verification
Shared component that wraps Poseidon3 for scope commitment; imported by both agent_policy and delegation circuits for consistency
No ABI changes needed (scopeCommitment remains a bytes32 field); update NatSpec comments to document that the commitment now includes a blinding salt
Update commitment builder to generate a cryptographically random 32-byte blindingSalt, compute Poseidon3 off-chain, and return {commitment, blindingSalt} for caller to store securely
Extend AgentPolicyInputs interface with blindingSalt: bigint field; update ProofInputs for delegation to include blindingSalt array per delegation hop
Circom unit tests: verify correct commitment output, verify proof fails with wrong salt, verify brute-force infeasibility by asserting ~2^16 bitmask enumeration cannot match commitment without salt
SDK unit tests: check blindingSalt is fresh on each call, check commitment determinism given same inputs, check round-trip proof generation and verification
Document the privacy threat model (bitmask brute-force attack), the fix rationale, the updated commitment schema, and key management guidance for blindingSalt storage
Migration guide: existing scope commitments must be reissued; enrollment flow changes; backward-incompatible note with upgrade path for live deployments

steps: Add scope_commitment.circom shared component with a Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt) template
Update agent_policy.circom: add `signal input blindingSalt` as private input, replace 2-ary Poseidon call with the shared Poseidon3 component
Update delegation.circom: add blindingSalt as private input per hop, thread it through chain-linking recomputation using the same shared component
Recompile circuits, regenerate R1CS and witness generation artifacts, confirm ~800-constraint increase vs baseline
Update SDK scopeCommitment.ts to generate a fresh 32-byte random salt via crypto.getRandomValues, compute Poseidon3 off-chain, and return {commitment, blindingSalt}
Extend SDK types (AgentPolicyInputs, ProofInputs) with the blindingSalt field
Update proof generation helpers to pass blindingSalt into snarkjs/witness generation
Write circuit unit tests covering: correct output, wrong-salt rejection, delegation chain consistency
Write SDK unit tests covering: salt freshness, determinism, proof round-trip
Update specs/scope-commitment-v2.md with threat model, fix rationale, and key management guidance
Write migration guide documenting the breaking change and re-enrollment requirement
Run full test suite; confirm no regressions in existing policy and delegation proof tests
dependencies: SDK Poseidon off-chain hasher (circomlibjs or equivalent)
secure randomness source in SDK runtime (crypto.getRandomValues or Node crypto)
